history_text:
This is Alice. She has a sister, a mother, a father, and before she became an undead abomination the most interesting thing about her was that she accidentally started a cult.

Alice was always fascinated by the occult. She had a sense that there was more to the world than what she’d been told. The day she learned that Santa was a beard and red suit placed upon many actors, she became obsessed with the idea that underneath the mask of reality, there lay entities moving amongst mortals for their own purposes.

It was the 90s, she was in her 20s, and was kind of a weirdo, so she turned to that forerunner of social media: internet forums.

There she would stay up far too late, talking about various mysterious sightings and her understanding of reality grew. It wasn’t just a hidden truth, but rather a prism of knowledge, from which a seeking light would refract into a thousand different truths. 

Someone on the forum started a private chatroom and she, being riddled with anxiety about everything and anything, threw herself into researching not only her own beliefs, but helping others chase down their own leads. Somehow, she became the default for questions, for knowing things, and her Purpose, of finding that prism of knowledge that would illuminate all others, became the group's Purpose. 

She also religiously backed up everything, and was very open about this fact. She became their record keeper.

She was drawn to one of the other members of the group. Finally, he managed to persuade her to travel to England, and meet with him for the turn of the century. Together, he wrote, they would discover great things. Although terrified of flying, she agreed, telling her family that she was off to England to study.

Mere days before she left, there was a hack. Who was responsible for it was never discovered, but it put the fear into the entire online group, and large sections of the forums, private chats etc were deleted. Everyone in the club has pieces of the original chat room, but only Alice has the key that will allow the entire log (including all the break out rooms and private chats) to be fully reassembled.

She said she would do so when she got back from her trip.

Then she disappeared.

Embraced mere moments before the ball dropped on the year 2000, she entered a torpor. When she awoke, it was to discover that her family thought she was dead, and the online group was still going strong. Her family were overjoyed to get messages from her but, due to the whole Australia situation, she was unable to return. 

Some phonecalls and (once the technology allowed it) video calls reassured them that she wasn’t some scammer pretending to be Alice, and she put down her reluctance to return to Australia as a now-justified greater fear of travel. It probably says something about her that her family, once they managed to scrape together enough money to visit her, didn’t find her much changed. She, naturally, couldn’t tell them what had happened to her.

The online group had, to her surprise, continued. In fact, in her absence, there has arisen a pseudo-magical belief in the community that there is some great truth that could only be fully comprehended when the chat logs she held are reassembled, not as they were originally, but in some different combination that would reveal what the human mind was never meant to know. 

Alice hasn't disclosed her change in living status, nor has she disclosed anything about her personal life. Bits and pieces might be picked up, and people have met up in real life, but always under pseudonyms and in strange places.

Knowing what she now did, she saw how useful this group of dedicated believers could be. Because her sire, who had spoken to her extensively before Embracing her about his belief that he was being hunted for getting too close to the truth… has gone missing.

Brandy LaRoux; born 1950 in the Bayou near Baton Rouge, Louisiana to parents Marie-Terese and Jean LaRoux. One of 9 children. 

The LaRoux family was of the swamp; bred in the thick filth and muck; and synonymous with the creeping creatures that lived in it. Impoverished and socially outcast, the large family lived in tight quarters in a small wooden shack that looked heavy in disrepair.

Her parents were prone to laziness, alcohol, and gambling. It made affairs of the home torrid. Though; money always did seem to be available for the necessities. Schooling was basic. Many of her siblings left before their 7th year to get employ. Still; Brandy was an avid devourer of information. Too bright for the swamp, some would say but she loved the land and the critters in it. 

Sundays were always at a local unimposing church. Catholic; but steeped in the colourful synchronicity of Voodoo. Brandy always loved her Sunday afternoon sessions with Emmylou; the preacher’s wife; who would teach the children of protective grisgris and gifts from the swamp in the form of healing herbs. There was always a community feed. 

Though life was not unhappy in her childhood years; and it was often filled with memories of frolicking in the swamp lands: hand trawling cat fish, fighting off opossums, digging up crawdads, catching fire flies, and just generally getting into mischief and sometimes something more sinister. As she became a mature age she resigned herself to life in the backwaters and curbed her ambitions of a more glamorous life.... 

But fate has a twisted sense of humour. Or perhaps that's just vampires. In any case; at the age of 28, on a blood moon night, Brandy found herself embraced into a whole new world. Her embrace, enacted by a tormenting Invictus Gangrel eager to impress his sire, was a twisted punishment against the vampire Yusuf. In fact, her entire life had  been the result of orchestrated events starting decades ago, unbeknownst to her. 

Her great grandmother had been the dark magical spawn of Yusuf and his Vampire bride. A dhampir. And her lineage steeped in history, had always been intended for the embrace. The spitting image of her great, great grandmother; who had a sick vanity, saw her genetic copy as a new play thing and planned to make her an agent of her own to which she could shape Brandy in her own image. Alas. Another Monster struck.

For the decades that followed, Brandy was caged, hunted and tortured by her sire and his "true" kin. A sport they delighted in. The suffering was immeasurable. And so, when Brandy befriended a Crone, and saw the horrors of Cruac... and saw that suffering could be for her own private gain and empowerment and not just the amusement of others. She said Yes. Show me the way Mother. Save me from my wretched life and tell me how to turn this pain to power and I will serve you until the end of days.

Name: Carver 
Clan: Venture
Bloodline: Lasombra
Covenant: Lancea Sanctum
Concept: Kindred Academic
Hobbies: 
Plays guitar (badly)
Loves punk rock
Collects vintage teaspoons
Background
Born in the 50s to a wealthy family in London, England
Family business: Father was a partner at a Law Firm, Mother was of a wealthy family, so professional socialite
Raised Anglican, but was more casual about it than his parents
Went to University at Cambridge to study Finance and Law
Left University and started a small Legal practice to assist low-income families in accessing affordable legal advice in South London, funded by his family as a Philanthropic exercise
Over a decade he grows his practice and reputation among the community, across all cross sections of humanity
He becomes a Barrister and continues his practice, subsidising his philanthropic work by making investments in charitable organisations
Discovered one of his Partners was using the firm to exploit his charities and clients, indulging various unsavoury predilections
Came in to the office one night to confront him in the act, but was overpowered by the Partner in a violent struggle and mortally wounded by gunshot to the chest
Awoke several evenings later as a Vampire
His sire, an Invictus of some renown in the City, explains that she rescued him from death as he was being groomed for the embrace, his family having been her direct descendants
Getting along in years, he welcomes the opportunity to cheat death and embraces being Kindred
He is “given” to the Lancea Sanctum by his sire, she explains as a tradition among her lineage
He adopts the slant on the Anglican Faith presented by the Sanctified, embracing his role as a “Holy Monster”, having a strong sense of Justice
Once it became obvious that he could no longer continue his mortal vocation, he devoted himself to more academic pursuits
History and Mythology soon became his passion, and he dedicated himself fully to his studies, recording stories and sifting them for evidence of Kindred and other supernatural intervention
While not a formal member of the Inquisition, less interested in “field work” often required by their Order, he is still consulted regularly as a recognised Scholar
In this study he also learns more of the history of the Lasombra, and their role in the Church. He chooses to focus his studies into how he might use those powers to better serve the Damned and their purpose
Over the following decade he collects and curates a substantive Academic and Occult library, as well as storing several artefacts
When expansion to Australia was discussed, given the nature of the events that had unfolded to cause the banishment of the supernatural from the land, he was directed by his Family to accept the posting in the “frontier”, nominally to learn more about the specifics, but also to establish a foothold for the Lasombra in a new country
Seeing it as an opportunity to gather more knowledge about the World of Darkness, he was happy to oblige, and moved to Sydney with a contingent of the First and Second Estate

Preceding Embrace (pre 12/03/1947) 
Edna Judge (born Edna Judith Thurgood) was, in life, the picture of a Latter Day Saint. Modest, soft spoken and maternal. Her grandparents fled persecution following the murder of Joseph Smith. Pure, delightsome stock. She excelled in her lessons in homemaking as a child and giving her testimonies to her father and to her local church.
Women had only recently been allowed to join missions, and it was still not encouraged for all. Not Edna. She wanted it known that she felt Heavenly Father so deeply that she could not be kept inside a home until she had her chance to get out into the world and spread his gospel. Her testimonies gave her an edge on her peers. Her father had a marriage lined up for her when she returned at 23 years old.
She never figured out why Heavenly Father chose to punish her in particular. She was so good. As a child her entire town knew her as a girl any man would be lucky to have and any woman would be lucky to have as a sister-wife. And that her children would be lucky to have such a righteous mother. She was Elect, she had always been told as much and she believed it too.
So when she became wife to Lehi Judge, why did she have no home to build? Why would Heavenly Father not bless her with even one child? She watched as her sisters surpassed her in her homemaking for their own families in the most material sense for years. Was it her drive to wait until after her mission that Heavenly Father decided she would not bear even one child?
Quietly in these years she quietly began to wonder about Heavenly Mother. As patient as Lehi was she could tell he was quietly resentful of the outlawing of polygamy. Perhaps Heavenly Father’s plan was for her to connect so deeply to Him that she might reach her Exaltation in the Celestial Kingdom. This must be a test. That she would not have any children on Earth in order to become more prepared than any other woman to be the perfect steady hand behind her husband as they progress eternally. Perhaps it was a gift.
She certainly believed it was so when the call for Lehi to relocate to Ohio as a Mission President. She could be of help here. She was free to assist him, this was where her progression on earth would truly begin. The Cincinnati mission would expand under Lehi’s leadership and she would be his support.
Hubris, of course. Her picturing herself one day as progressing to embody Heavenly Mother for the planet in Lehi’s celestial future, quiet as it was, must have angered Heavenly Father. She clearly did not heed the warning he sent her in her infertility, so he sent her a message she could not ignore.
Gwylim Lloyd was the vampire sent by Heavenly Father to sever her ties to Heavenly Mother forever. He presented himself to the missionaries under Lehi’s charge as a Golden Investigator, and he played his part perfectly. Not a hint of his plan was revealed until he was to meet with Lehi himself.
Edna sat in on that fateful meeting between Lloyd and Lehi that night. Aging though she was, her appearance was meticulously maintained and she knew that a dutiful and doting wife was a selling point for any serious male investigator.
1947 - 2026
Gwylim Lloyd had a plan for Lehi that she has never been sure if she is lucky to be brought along with. Lloyd saw the raw, mortal power of Lehi’s proselytising and decided he should be called to serve eternally. His offer was that Lehi would never see the Celestial Kingdom but would serve here on earth instead. That he could remain in the Terrestrial Kingdom for his eternal progression under Lloyd. Lehi might have been swayed if Lloyd had not made the mistake of phrasing this offer as repentance for his belief in Mormon Doctrine, and that this would be at Old St Mary’s Church, as Catholic as they come in that region.
It was for this reason that Lehi could not be embraced gently. Generations of true Latter Day Saint stock, he was alert to accusations of heresy. It is how he managed in all other cases to navigate his proselytising in a city so heavily contested in Christian doctrines, and how he trained his missionaries to do the same.
The vampire had already decided that he would have him though. And Edna was to accompany him. She was sure all along that this was a sick joke from Gwylim. That if her purpose was to be his wife then she should do so. After all, that was going to be the plan anyway, right? She always wondered how he must have felt when Lehi did not survive The Change. Was he truly so resentful of only walking out of that office with a woman that he would not take the time to learn the use she could be to him?
It was a violent attack, and everything in her soul fought against it. Her grip on Heavenly Father is the only thing that pulled her through, she is sure of it. She did not learn that her husband had not made it with her for some weeks, and Lloyd put her right to work. She was kept under Old St Mary’s in those early days. Lloyd would bring sinners down to her before he fed. He would not feed from them before their blood was “purified”.
In a ritual he called “sin-eating”, she would drip a small amount of their blood into a chalice, and drink it over their unconscious bodies. Prayer beforehand, prayer ending. Never quite enough to satiate her. Never frequent enough that she was not hungry.
Her only solace was the sister who kept her. A kind kindred woman whose name she never learned. It was this woman that told Edna of her husband’s passing, and that he was buried on the grounds. I think she meant this as some kind of comfort - that he was close - but it had at this point been weeks since Edna had seen the sky. She was weak, she could feel her young beast railing against her ribcage with as much as it could muster. The news broke her, and the remaining shreds that kept her from transforming into a creature of need rather than one of needs and wants snapped.
But the sister cared for her that year. She became more animal in spirit, yes, but the sister washed her clean of spilled blood, brushed and braided her hair, kept her bedroll clean and changed lightbulbs when they blew. She was gentle, and she was kind enough to open the door to the mausoleum that housed Edna on the night she smelled the smoke.
Edna doesn’t know what Lloyd did to attract hunters, and has never quite cared to find out. He was destroyed, she was sure of that. She could feel it, but it wasn’t her priority. Survival was the only thing that mattered.
She doesn’t like to talk about the decade that followed. The only things that mattered by the time the 70s were approaching was that Lehi had been exhumed from the grounds of Old St Mary’s and reburied in Tennessee where she resided in those years. He would follow her wherever she moved, and she would tend his grave forever. That, she had decided. She doesn’t know if the nature of his death would have closed the gates to the celestial kingdom on him, but she was determined to be by his side, caring for him for eternity as Heavenly Father intended.
In Tennessee she found a chapter of the Lancea Et Sanctum that, while Protestants, she found she could blend with and get along with. Mormonism was not so different to the way kindred practiced their faith under Him. Living forever as perfected beings, with marked improvements in their abilities after their embrace. They could all agree that they were doomed for what she agreed to refer to as “hell”, rather than the Telestial Kingdom, but they would serve Him on the Terrestrial Kingdom, saving fallen gentiles that they might not share a sunless fate.
Times were changing in the 70s; women were expected to work. And work she did. Where the Lancea et Sanctum found best use for her was something of a boogeyman. She had avoided thinking about her appearance for many years as to be delightsome is to be closer to Heavenly Father. And she was no longer delightsome. Gwylim had hid his true countenance to her throughout his plotting, one she had inherited as his Childe. Another punishment for something or other from Heavenly Father, surely, but not one she could bring herself to confront.
Instead, she found herself able to use it as a tool, as part of what they were calling an “Employee Incentive Program” in those days. Those more personable than her would target higher ups in firms and companies increasing in power and wealth. They would poke and prod around. If the gentiles were already drenched in sin, they needed only to be sent to her. Being closer to Heavenly Father would benefit their business. The Lancea et Sanctum would make sure of it, and she would be the monster in the closet to show exactly what the power of Heavenly Father could do to those who defy his will. A macabre confession where her sin eating ritual once again became useful, although not strictly required.
In between clients she fed on her herd. Maintained immaculately as The Sister - saint she was - maintained her under Old St Mary’s. Much easier for someone with her countenance to keep some food at home. Those who, while not as powerful as her clients, had returned to Heavenly Father through her guidance. And they had no opportunities to sin, which comforted her. If she had children as she thought she might back then, she would also want them to live free of the sin she felt coursing through her.
Tennessee in those days was firmly in the hands of the Lancea et Sanctum, a Praxis they had fought for in the shadows increasing their power by way of influencing the gentiles in power to do what would turn the masses to God. The suits would make living for the average fallen man so difficult that He was the only salvation. The more the mortal congregation grew, the stronger the Lance had a hold on the state. They rode the recession following the Vietnam war as one rides a Hymn’s melody. 
It didn’t last forever but it was glorious while it did. She remembers those days fondly. Once inflation was under control their power began to wane. She had heard whispers of the same across the bible belt. But she had also spent years then downplaying her true faith in Heavenly Father. Calling him “God”, speaking of “Heaven” and “Hell”. Never mentioning Joseph Smith.
The years since then have not quite been much to speak of in comparison. As the decades rolled on she hopped from state to state, reconnecting with Heavenly Father and quietly reaching out for Heavenly Mother. Her time with the Protestants had lit a curiosity in her about other sects of Abrahamic faiths where she used to feel a pious resentment, so she read, and read, and read. Scriptures considered canon in their respective faiths, scriptures considered apocryphal. She would read passages to Lehi wherever he had been buried at the time, wondering if he would have found it to be as interesting had he shared these decades with her. She suspected not, but she always felt more human connecting with him in this way.
She also kept up with Mormon doctrine and leaders, and while she did not quite have the acquired for what Utah had become, she kept abreast of it’s flourishing as the Mormon capital of the world. She read its literature and watched as its goods and culture seemed to creep and then boom into popularity. Twilight was strange, but surprisingly not as inaccurate in many ways that others would frame it, and oh she would debate this point with her brothers and sisters of the cloth when she arrived in NOLA.
NOLA is where Edna had most recently resided. Carthian run city, but steadily increasing as a red state since the turn of the century, which had boded well for The Lancea Sanctum in the past. Unfortunately here it seemed the Invictus also had their eyes and hopes set on a similar goal.
She could not quite get an operation of a similar calibre to Tennessee running in here with it so hotly contested. Not as one of the only newcomers on the scene. The lack of direction and distraction wasn’t all to healthy for her. New Orleans was where a descendent of hers was residing. About as old now as she was upon her embrace. And Ellen fascinated her. She has been watching her from afar for years. Ex-mormon, divorced, “child-free” and independently wealthy. And seemingly… Happy. Edna watches her when she catches up with her friends, drinking wine of all things and eating beignets.
Edna tries not to wonder what this could mean for Ellen, much less what it could have meant for her.

Einar Solvieg was born in 1803 in a small coastal town just outside Copenhagen. Working as a dock labourer through his teens - it was not long before he sought adventure abroad and signed up as a sailor with the Dutch East India Company doing trips between Scandinavia, Europe, and the America's.

In 1845, the ship on which Einar served was targeted by a crew of Rötgrafen Vampires and their Ghouls. When the dust settled, the undead pirates were looking for some new recruits, and Einar had something about him that their captain liked. Her name was Olga Anderson, and she gave him the option of becoming a part of her crew, living an immortal life of adventure on the open seas, or having his throat cut, his lifeblood drained, and going down with the ship. For Einar, it was not a particularly difficult decision.

The Rötgrafen don't care much for the ways of civilized Kindred, and Einar's embrace was not sanctioned by any Prince. He was turned, and locked in the brig along with several of the surviving crew from the captured vessel. When he awoke, he fed upon and slaughtered his old comrades in his newborn hunger, solidifying his place among his new companions through the destruction of his old life. 

In the wake of many raids and an age-old grudge - the crew spent a lot of time at sea, moving nomadically from place to place and avoiding the attention of the Lancea Sanctum whose crusaders had hunted the Rötgrafen for centuries.

Eternity is a long time, however, and wood rots, sails tear, and eventually repairs need to be made. Einar and Olga’s crew found themselves having to lay over in San Francisco for an extended period in 1907, where the Lance finally caught up with them. With most of the crew staked or destroyed and their home ablaze around him - a loyal ghoul spent his last moments on the earth shoving Einar's injured corpse into the lead-lined chest where he normally rested during the sunlight hours, locking it tight against the fire, intrusion, or the sun as the ship plunged to the bottom of the sea.

Einar lay in Torpor on the bottom of the ocean for almost half a century, undisturbed until 1960 when some maritime archeologists investigated the wreck and discovered the locked chest. Thinking they had found some valuable pirate treasure, they eagerly took it home, and cracked it open in secret. They probably shouldn't have done that.

And so Einar found himself in LA in the 1960's - alone, weakened, without friends, without status, without deeds to his name and with very little experience of civilized Vampire culture. Einar made friends where he could, did jobs for whoever would accept him. He wandered from city to city, hitchhiked across America, and even stowed away on cargo ships when the wanderlust took him. He made his way back to Europe and travelled around Scandinavia, visiting the places where he grew up and his ancestral home.

Drawn to the London night-life in the 70’s, partying in Amsterdam through the 80’s, and rarely staying still for very long - Einar never put down roots - constantly looking over his shoulder in anticipation of the Crusaders who wiped out his crew tracking him down.

It was thus that he found himself attracted to the Carthian Movement, as despite his advanced age he had nothing to draw upon to establish power, enforce his will, or support his claim to the Blood. His Requiem he been dominated by a nomadic lifestyle of freedom and self-determination, which form the backbone of his personal philosophy, and which has allowed him to find a home in The Movement.

Einar has strong cultural ties to his past life and people - embracing Norse Paganism as his personal conviction, and reveling in the mythology of his bloodline, believing himself to be possessed of the blood of Loki's children.

Having spent a large portion of his Requiem as a literal nomadic pirate and mercenary - he is a hedonist with a competitive streak, and has a natural tendency to turn his nose up at authority.

Jack 'Slick' Fallow was born in 1942 in San Mateo County in California, from a wealthy family with three siblings. He was a drama kid -- loved acting, and had roles in all the school plays. He joined the photography club as a sophomore and the Yearbook club in his final year. As a young man in his 20s, he lived through what he still thinks is the height of cinema in the 1960s: To Kill a Mockingbird, 2001 A Space Odyssey, Psycho, Midnight Cowboy, Easy Rider, Breakfast at Tifafny's and Lolita.  He idolized Stanley Kubrick, Alfred Hitchcock and Roman Polanski, Fellini, Kurosawa and Bergman. 

His breakout project as a director was a low budget avant garde short film which earned him a showing at the San Francisco Short Film Festival in 1967 where he rubbed elbows with the likes of David Lynch and Andy Warhol, and he even saw George Lucas' first film, The Electronic Labyrinth, while he was a student at USC. While Jack's film didn't win any awards, it caught the attention of a wealthy patron from San Diego who bankrolled some further projects. These were all amazing productions -- but ended up as duds at the box office for one reason or another. Jack couldn't believe his bad luck -- post-production (especially marketing) fucked him at every turn. 

All the while, he watched the great films come out in the 70s:  Taxi Driver, One Flew over the Cuckoo's Nest, The Godfather, Exorcist, Jaws, Carrie, Saturday Night Fever, Superman (with Christopher Reeves) and Star Wars A New Hope. He admired but resented those great directors (Francis Ford Coppola, Steven Spielberg or John Carpenter), knowing he would never be one of them. 

This never worked out as planned. 

Refusing help from his family, he got whatever work he could, doing shitty short films and commercials, and eventually found his way into porn.  That went amazingly well...until it didn't.   

It was a life of drugs, sex and parties throughout the Golden Age of the San Fernando Valley adult film industry, from 77 to 84.  And then it went downhill fast as the medium switched from cinemas to hand-held video camcorders. Amateur garbage.  [Inspired from Boogie Nights].  

Jack hit is lowest point in 1987, on Hallows Eve, when he decided to end his life. It was then that he met Jarod Edwards II, his Sire, who listened to his story and then, in an act of supreme cruelty, deprived Jack of his release by Embracing him.  

Jarod impressed upon Jack almost daily that he had failed as a human being and it was now time to acknowledge that he has a chance to begin again...as a monster that feeds on lesser homo sapiens. The brainwashing was effective, with Jack only able to remember his humanity (and experience a shred of it) through film, through characters on a screen, second-hand. 

His first kill was horrible. The eyes of his victim still haunt Jack. Now, whenever he feeds, Jack blindfolds his victims, just in case he messes up and kills them by over-feeding. He never looks his victims in the eye while he feeds. 

In time, Jack was brought fully into the Moulding Room, and eventually given his assignment:  go to Sydney and advance the mission. Open territory. Lay claim.

____________* Kirk Grimm’s Tale *____________

Seeing the Irish Civil War, and the atrocities committed by the IRA, Kirk’s father moved to Ireland from England and joined the Royal Irish Constabulary (RIC), otherwise known as the “Black and Tans”. As a young man, Kirk idolised his father and the struggle for control over Ireland against the rebels, and naively vowed one day to ‘protect’ Ireland himself. In his youth, he resented the Irish for not siding with the Catholic Church, who he was told tacitly supported the Nazis in Germany, and resisted conscription on those grounds.

As the social unrest that would lead to the Troubles occurred in Belfast, Kirk felt it was his turn to fight against the social degradation of the Irish. He followed his increasingly conservative father, and joined a fledgling organisation within the Ulster Defence Association (UDA) he simply knew as “The Cavalry”. While he wanted to protect Irish Protestants and re-unify Ireland under English rule, his father coaxed him towards more and more violent action. Increasingly radicalised, Kirk was Embraced and put under Vinculum by his father, who became a leader figure within the Tollisian heretic splinter cell in Belfast, as a Gangrel Paladin of the Crimson Cavalry. As part of his initiation prior to his Embrace, he burned down a Catholic monastery as a show of faith, and committed many such crimes against kine and kindred after, and worse.

The Crimson Cavalry sent Kirk on many missions but primarily fielded him against the Lancea et Sanctum proper, ‘gelding’ him as a Sorcerous Eunuch so he could better hunt Theban Sorcerers. He was told nearly nothing of the Dance Macabre, and what he was told was nearly entirely lies and half-truths. The Catholics had forsaken the Light, he was told, and worshiped a Dark Messiah that raped his own mother, and empowered his unholy followers to use heinous blood magic. 

It was not just the Lance in Belfast, however. Funding the Cavalry’s efforts, the Invictus wanted to leverage the chaos for their own ends, and Kirk was also sent to harass and persecute the Carthians supporting the cause of Irish resistance. Specifically, Macheath, a Mekhet Cathian front-line combatant, crossed paths with Kirk more than once, and a quiet respect for the idealistic nature of the shadowy adversary.

However, as the Troubles began in earnest, Kirk became increasingly disquieted by the racially and sex-preference discriminatory aspects of the Crimson Cavalry’s doctrine. His resolve broke when he was cajoled by his father to murder a pregnant Jewish kine, a refugee from the Holocaust, purely for “racial purity” of the kine herd rather than feeding. Distraught by his actions, and with the Vinculum to his father waned, Kirk fled the heretic paramilitary and hid from his father and the rest of the Cavalry by travelling to London, trying to keep a low profile and avoid ongoing Trouble’s chaos.

However, a ferocious, fanatic lupine was present in the area and were in serious danger of revealing the supernatural World of Darkness to the kine with how bold their movements were, causing an Inquisitor of the Lancea et Sanctum by the name of Conrad Sondergaard to come across Kirk as a suspect, given his canine aspect. Taking the Childe under his wing, the young Inquisitor was surprised by his combat experience and tenacity, and allowed Kirk to track the werewolf and cast it out, along with the parishes’ Paladins and Crusaders. When pushed on his parish and sire, Conrad enlisted Kirk to join him in holding the Crimson Cavalry to account as penance for his sins.

Travelling to Belfast with Inquisitor Sondergaard, Kirk dedicated himself to hunting the heretics who inducted him to the night. With Conrad’s expertise and experience, Kirk’s bitter contempt for his old coterie was inexorable. Weakened as the Cavalry were by repeatedly violating the Tradition of Progeny to expand their ranks, the Inquisitor and his retinue cut a bloody swathe through the heretic kindred, until Kirk faced down his sire. Having been trained in the way of the Crusader by the Lancea et Sanctum, Kirk caught his sire unaware, and pulverised him to unrecognisablity. With his sire’s last strength, he used the last vestiges of his Vinculum to commanded Kirk to diablerise him, cementing his damnation and assuring  his culling by his newfound faith, his final conquest over his wayward Childe.

Conrad, however, arrived at the last opportune moment, and literally kicked Kirk off his sire like a misbehaving mongrel. Strangling his Frenzy, Kirk cowed to his compatriot, and allowed his sire to be staked and hauled away to the Lances’ darkest cells. Who Embraced his father was never known to Kirk, and though they searched, they could not find head nor tail of the Cavalry’s arch-heretic in Ireland. He may still be out there, waiting to reclaim his childe’s childe, and restart his heinous reign of heretical rule.

Since that night, Kirk Grimm has been Inquisitor Sondergaard’s baleful shadow. A hound of heaven, a church grim, a perros de guerra for the Lancea et Sanctum covenant, being the personal paladin for his “Boss”. They have not tangled with lupine again, but Kirk still keeps a mundane bastard sword as a trophy of that fight, and yearns to test his mettle against such a beast again. More often, The Inquisitor and his Mongrel dally with the other covenants, more often the Carthians and Circle of the Crone. Kirk despises the witchlings, being the actual embodiment of what he thought he saw in the Lance, but has a quiet respect for the Carthians. Kirk has crossed paths with many Carthians in London, especially one René, who is like him, a church-burner; he may be the one person in the Lance they could find a sympathetic ear with. He, unlike most Lance, is uneasy around the First Estate, for their mercenary attitudes are alien to him.

For feeding, Kirk tends to foster connections with young, disadvantaged young men as something of a Christian “Scene Queen”. He sees his former self in his prey, and though he refuses to admit it to himself in his self-loathing celibacy, his sexual preferences tend towards them as well. A large, gruff, silent man with quiet zealotry, such youths gravitate to him for his understated ‘realness’ in amongst the sickly-sweet rhetoric of the kine’s theology. Small Bible-studies, faith retreats and especially men’s sheds form the basis of his feeding rituals, disguising his feeding as rapturous benediction of the Holy Spirit within private, one-on-one prayer circles. 

The Disciplines he uses include his Protean abilities to meld with the earth outside of his charges’ lodgings, in his way of standing guard, even during the daylight hours. Less versed in Animalism than many Gangrel (having lived almost exclusively in the hearts of cities), Kirk’s true strengths are is his Resilience and combat training. While he has his own, understated faith, his lack of confidence in speaking, as well as his turbulent past, means preaching on the minutia of theology is not in his future, barring extraordinary nights ahead. The one time his conviction shows itself is when he bears his Beast; roaring Scripture and fiery condemnation as the righteous indignation and holy rage, like a powder-keg ignited. He keeps the Beast at bay with a Human racked by guilt and shame. He has a perilously low Humanity, and is without a living Touchstone.

Kirk’s duties in Sydney are twofold. Firstly, the protection of his Inquisitor, in general. He’s been inseparable from
Conrad for decades, viewing him as his spiritual leader, intellectual superior and benevolent benefactor. Secondly, he wishes to connect with other Gangrel. His Beast is a cross he struggles to bear, and wants their knowledge in managing this burden.

Und der Haifisch, der hat Zähne,
Und die trägt er im Gesicht.
Und Macheath, der hat ein Messer,
Doch das Messer sieht man nicht.
-Brecht, Threepenny Opera

***

His name isn’t really Macheath, or even Mac. His sire gave him that name, and sung him her favourite verses. It was the first German he learned. 

***

His father was Catholic, from a Fenian family that owned almost nothing beyond their pride. His mother’s family was Protestant; not wealthy but very comfortable. They disowned her when she married a Catholic boy and converted, so he never knew those grandparents, aunts, uncles or cousins.

His early years were typical; chores started before dawn, then a long walk to school and running home for more chores in the afternoon. The oldest of a large brood, he took his responsibility for his brothers and sisters very seriously. Meals were meagre, but his father would tell stories of the Easter Rising and sing songs that promised a free and equal Ireland without British tyranny. On Saturdays, he’d help his father, doing odd jobs and fixing broken furniture to sell. Sundays were church, and sometimes playing with other children outside while the men had quiet, serious conversations with the priest.

His father pushed him to work hard at school; he took to almost nothing academic naturally but found that he could just work harder than his classmates. Perhaps he could be a teacher, or an engineer. At the worst, his mother told him, he could get a good trade. Perhaps work as a carpenter like Jesus and make beautiful things from Irish oak. 

In the summer of 1942, two drunken British soldiers followed his father home and beat him with a metal rod until they had covered the floor in teeth and blood. The attack was covered up by the local RUC as two brave soldiers defending themselves from a bitter old Fenian. The blood stains on the floorboards never washed out. Mac’s father would never walk or speak again, having suffered a series of strokes after his skull was cracked and most of his face crushed to powder. 

That was the end of studying and any dreams of a better life. Mac left school, took any work he could find. In the evenings, he swept pub floors, ran errands or stood lookout for petty criminals. While his brothers and sisters learned their letters, he helped his mother clean houses. They used her maiden name; no Catholic family they knew could afford cleaners, and wealthy Protestants only employed co-religionist Unionists. It was Mac’s first encounter with the power of a name, and he learned the lesson quickly. 

The first time he found military orders amid a pile of discarded papers he sold them to a man from church. Within a week, he was sitting down with an IRA commander. His father was a hero, did he know that? He’d fought for a free Ireland and the enemy had savaged him for it. It would right to help the cause, wouldn’t it lad? To get back at the bastards. Of course, he shouldn’t tell his mother. She’d only worry. 

For an angry boy who already saw himself as a man, it was intoxicating. He learned to box, and to fight dirty, but his real value to the cause was his access to the homes of Loyalists. With the right name, deferentially downcast eyes and a mop, attention would just slide right past. 

The first man Mac killed, John Reilly, wasn’t English, or even a Protestant. He was an informer, a Catholic turncoat. Reilly never suspected a 14-year old selling yesterday’s newspapers, and Mac had no idea that the gun would be so loud. He fled, ears still ringing, gun still smoking. The older men clapped him on the shoulder and praised his bravery. A year later, while fireworks celebrated German surrender in Europe, Mac was stabbing an Ulster militiaman through the neck with a stolen bayonet. 

He grew too big to squirm through holes or pass as his mother’s helper, so his tactics changed. Calm under pressure and patient, he learned that strong and steady hands could kill a man as reliably as a bullet without the noise. A man with a gun might be searched and executed; a man with nothing but a bucket and mop who stank of disinfectant was waved through. He kept count and names of the men he killed. He thinks so anyway. The years and blood blurred. He led men sometimes; not an inspiring leader but a practical one.

By the ‘60s, as The Troubles began to gather like storm clouds, he was making wider connections. He travelled as an escort for arms shipments from Spain and America; he met young fighters from Germany and Italy and Lebanon. He was patched up by a beautiful young doctor who put in the most artful stitches he’d seen and then belted him when he made a pass at her. 

In 1965, he met Karoline Lenya. A young West German Marxist, she soon revealed herself as a vampire. Recently made herself, she offered him a name and a new talent. She told him to grow his hair and beard, supposedly for a temporary disguise, and with a flagrant disregard for the Masquerade, taught him the basics of unlife. One night, drink on cheap whiskey and Guinness laced with oily vitae, he accepted the offer, and died. 

It took decades for Mac to really come to terms with Karoline’s manipulation, but she brought him into the Movement and into Kindred society. He discovered that her Norvegi blood placed him in the same position amongst vampires he’d been born into as a child, his fangless countenance marking him as lesser, as detestable. The Carthians in Northern Ireland, much like in West Germany, were aligned with mortal militant groups who were increasingly striking out at institutions that had been Invictus strongholds. It was easy enough to motivate him, and he learned to harness his strange blood against enemies old and new. 

Karoline released him quickly, returning to Germany and leaving a string of childe in her wake.

He met Doctor Kane again when her sire introduced her. Soon enough she was Doc, and sometimes even Maggie. They worked together well, and after he was almost ripped apart in a back alley showdown with the Lance attack dog Kirk Grim, Maggie stitched him back up for the second time, and belted him again for good measure. But he made another pass and that time she didn’t bother belting him. 

They became inseparable, and known as a pair of reliable troubleshooters. Maggie handled politics and build alliances; Mac kept her safe from guns and blades in the dark. Maggie taught him to sleep in the earth, and he taught her how to read a street. When they needed to, they’d ambush enemies in back alleys or dump them into freshly poured foundations. They were good at it. 

They travelled sometimes, meeting the Red Army Faction at Karoline’s request, and smuggled weapons from Egypt for the Provos back home. Life was never relaxed, which suited Mac just fine. 

Maggie was always the leader, and he preferred it that way. Those who knew him knew his worth, and Carthians from Cork to Belfast learned that if you had an Invictus problem, the sharp-tongued Kerberos and looming Norvegi were worth calling. 

In 1995, Karoline publicly attacked and diablerised an Invictus elder. The Movement couldn’t condone her behaviour, and Mac felt her slide into torpor long before he knew why. 

A year later, the Accords were signed. Most of Northern Ireland cheered. For Mac, who had lived in the shadow of the struggle as both mortal and kindred, it felt like a betrayal. The next night when the Movement and the Invictus announced their own ceasefire, Maggie told him that she’d be on the new committee that would organise the new politics of the nation, and he felt like he lost everything that gave him purpose. Warning her that working with the Invictus would be a disaster, he walked away. 

For the next decade, he avoided kindred life as much as he could. He discovered that the Movement’s ethos of collective action could be turned against a Carthian dissenter as easily as an Invictus, so he had to avoid his former havens and feeding grounds. At the same time, the might of the IRA was disintegrating. Drained by perpetual conflict with the monolith of the British Army, most Provos were ready for peace. The militant factions splintered and shattered like cheap glass, and soon enough Mac’s mortal connections were more like organised crime than organised resistance. His mortal family, held together by his mother’s fierce devotion, went to the four winds when she died - Dublin, Derry, Liverpool, Australia. 

In the mid 2000s, Maggie saved him for the third time. Burning her bridges in spectacular fashion, she excoriated the greedy and dissolute Kindred now comfortably acting as Northern Ireland’s new landlords, and left a letter in an old dead drop. For the first time in years, he had a purpose again. 

Leaving Ireland behind, they travelled. The ‘official’ story was smuggling - arms, antiquities, Kindred - but the real purpose was starting fires. Local Carthians learned that when Doc and Mac were in town, it was time to shake the tree. Mac wasn’t much of Carthian Lawyer, but he’d learned a few tricks that could turn a gilded crown into a royal noose. Whatever local resistance there was suddenly found themselves able to shift the balance of power, and if there wasn’t a resistance yet, Maggie made sure there was one by the time they left. 

As rumours began to reach them about Kindred returning to Australia, they couldn’t help but be curious. When the message came through that a young Carthian upstart was building something new and needed muscle, it was too good an opportunity to miss. A new continent, a new night. 

***

Denn die einen sind im Dunkeln
Und die andern sind im Licht
Und man siehet die im Lichte
Die im Dunkeln sieht man nicht.

history_text:
René Meyer
Player: Luca venn
Clan: Daeva
Bloodline: Zelnari 
Covenant: Carthian
Backstory
Mortal Life
Born in 1960's France to an artist and a WW2 veteran, René's family were staunch socialists who still felt the shadow of Vichy occupation. She moved to London in the 80's, having been fascinated with its artistic scene and burgeoning Anarchic subculture. Finding work as a union representative following in her parents footsteps she was quickly up to her neck in Thatcheresque politics.
Unknowingly René had arrived at a time of intense kindred conflict- following a great decline in Invictus influence in the city, the Carthians and Lancea sanctum were starting to compete more fiercely for control of domain. René had unwittingly positioned herself at a fulcrum of control over an industrial district of London that had a burgeoning anti-establishmentarianist movement, and had caught the eye of a kindred. A Carthian in the barest terms and a Daeva in the most vibrant, Oscar flew though unlife frivolously. Managing to find success when he needed it and excuses when he didn’t. René was an opportunity to solidify his hold on the city- and, more importantly, someone to offload the bothersome workings of politicking whilst he reveled. 
She mightn’t have the ‘it’ factor that he would prefer as a Zelani, but her plucky rabble-rousing and back-room dealing had a certain charm that had no risk in upstaging him. Posing as a philanthropic benefactor who was interested in funding her campaign against labor exploitation and stoked the fires of her passion towards his own purposes.. 
The final cherry in her coffin, he granted upon her the power of ‘The Wish’- and for three days René achieved the work of months. Negotiations that had taken days of hard labor now cut down with a smile, she could see when people were leading her on and led them on in turn. When those days were up, she was completely devoted to Osacar and accepted the blessing of his embrace. 
Early Undeath
René was immediately set to work solidifying her sire’s hold on the movement- any ideological musings forgotten. Gradually she deconstructed the foundations of work she and her colleagues had spent months on, repurposing them as a platform whereby Oscar could dictate his whims.
Soon enough her purposes evolved past mere organisation. The conflict between Lancea and the Carthian Movement had grown with the decline of the Invictus and her Sire, though a touch of planning and his signature habit of fortune uncovered (Or perhaps ensured) that an important Lance figure was captured by mortal hunters. Obviously this kindred had to be disposed of for ‘the sake of the masquerade’ and René was to be the one to do it. Difficult work, considering her newfound inability to enter uninvited, but her sire had hired a Nosferatu named ‘Charlie’ who managed to get them both inside. Perhaps Oscar meant this to be her true introduction to kindred life, as she mostly just watched as Charlie committed the bloody work.
As years progressed her enamourment with Oscar soured. In death she had been undoing the work of her life, vigorous political movements swept up for the purposes of kindred politicking. Art and ideas touched by dead hands quickly warped and drained of all life and potential. René grew cynical, suspecting the failings of many innovative mortal projects could be traced to kindred short-sightedness, despite the promises of the Carthian Movement. 
On a job for her Sire, dealing with an uncooperative political figure in what she suspected to be the seediest brothel in London, she was offered a release for this cynicism.
It was supposed to be a simple negotiation, the final bastard in a line of stubborn council members. Catching him as he was leaving a private room, she pushed him back inside whilst trying to bring to bear her most womanly of charms- supernaturally powered as they were, the man was an obstinate fellow, and she had a short temper. It only took his first attempt at violence before she took her second approach, and a meal. 
Stepping out of the room to think how she was going to clean up her mess, her beast roused her to a yet unnoticed presence standing in the long-empty hall. A nosferatu- she could not identify the face, but there was no question. Assuring they meant no harm, they explained they were aware of René’s position and feelings towards her work. They were here to offer help.
Not all Carthians had such meddlesome a view on mortal ideological development, the Anti-Obstructionist Army was dedicated to working against circumstances very much like René’s own embrace- though the cause she’d labored towards as a mortal was lost, there were others she might take up. 
Reinvigoration of purpose, it was a tempting offer. René was reticent towards tempting offers ever since she’d discovered the cost she paid for accepting her Sire’s. The Nosferatu affirmed that if she agreed, they’d not only save her from her Sire's shadow, but grant her means in which to resist ensorcaled compulsion. A secret that had been stolen during conflict with the Sanctified.
Preferring the unknown devil, she accepted and partook of the Nosferatu’s blood as a sign of pact- those drops of Vitae relighting the fire of her advocacy. 
Satisfied, the Nosferatu departed and René returned to the work of arranging the scene of her kill. The drug paraphernalia strewn across the room made overdose an easy sell. In the midst of her frame job, the steady beat of a heart tingled at her Kindred senses. There was someone else in the room, and the wet cough from the pile of clothes in the corner confirmed it. A sliver of a bruised up face, pupils wide and barely conscious. From the looks of it, the dead man on the bed was far from innocent. 

Unresponsive, her eyes still tracked René as she moved around the room… Which prevented a dilemma. Her work here was a Masquerade risk… but she had just made a commitment to a new cause. To shed mortal blood so quickly after felt like it would sully it, and likewise she couldn’t make a ghoul. 
It was a low-class brothel, in a shitty, out of the way part of town; drugs were rife here, and so was illegal activity, and everyone knew it. Even if this witness survived her injuries, she would be the antithesis of reliable. Besides, she had inherited her Sire’s luck- she could afford to gamble.
Placing a finger to her mouth in a gesture of silence, she winked - and though there was no response or flicker of recognition in the escort's eyes, she was pretty confident they got the idea. With that she walked from the Brothel, and towards the cause that would now encompass her unlife.
Anti-Obstructionist Initiation
Radical activism proved more difficult than expected. The restraint of mortal society wasn’t present in the political moves of kindred. Though the AOA had made good on their promise of distancing her Sire’s influence, going so far as to teach her techniques of subtly, and warding her against sorcery. With these gifts she was expected to prove herself capable of handling the more bloody work required of her.
That chance came with work in Ireland. She was to cut her fangs removing a ghoul who had integrated themselves with local political movements, using their influence to advance the desire of their dominator. A vitae-imbued stubborn veteran of the Troubles, gentle manipulations would prove useless. Force was needed, something her colleagues of the AOA were likely using to judge her commitment. René was no stranger to violence, but had never confronted a human aware of her nature. This lack of experience caused her to waiver in her coercion, which gave the man a chance to detonate ordinance he’d squirled away during his veterancy. She saw him dead before he could escape, at the cost of the house exploding around her.

Her luck buried her in the rubble, safe from the killing day- it didn’t go so far as to stop her limbs from being blown off. 
Saving grace from the tomb of this isolated cottage came from the kindred Macheath. Also having had business with the ghoul he’d found her the following night. Recognising her as Carthian he dragged her back to his coterie partner Doc, who started on rousing her from torpor. Awakened, René was at a disadvantage; Following some discussion (and a promise to put her legs back on) she explained her purpose for visiting the emerald isle and offered them both a favor in thanks, and to encourage their discretion. She returned to England successful, if not for some help, that she would not be mentioning. 
Having demonstrated her use, René was given a less subtle of tasks- there was a kindred cult operating in London, that was drawing kine in and warping their minds to a more pleasing tone. René now needed to show she was ready to step on toes a little closer to home.
She prepared in a very similar manner to her clandestine activism in her mortal days, covering her body and preparing a duffel bag full of assorted paints. To the outside eye, it would look like a simple bit of vandalism (If a little extreme), but to those in the know this headquarters would be marked by the AOA- and that they would need to be more careful with their interactions with the breathing masses if they were to avoid further reprisal. 

Breaking windows, and trashing furniture with supernatural strength- she had to admit it was quite cathartic. She could glimpse now the superiority that her Sire had always emanated, the careless surety of daring-do. In her revelry, she grew careless, and as she sensed the arrival of other kindred she made a mental note of the danger in giving into such careless carousal, lest she lose sight of what was actually around her… or the cause she pursued.
The Fire of Saint Leons
The political climate in London had soured. The Carthian Movement and Lancea Scanctum were practically at war, waged with mortal institutions. This tension was most obvious during a protest inflamed by allegations against a priest of the Church of Saint Leon. 
The legitimacy of these allegations were irrelevant, only that they were a useful tool to the Carthians; To destabilize mortal opinion of the church was to strike a blow against the Lancea Sanctum. 
René assisted the protest, acting as the hand of Carthian influence. Riling the crowd when it needed riling, and directing it when it became unfocused. It reminded her of her mortal days- it was the sort of movement she would join. She tried to ignore thinking about how many events she might’ve attended that were truly for only kindred use. 
Standing opposed to her in a line of constables was Conrad Sondergaard. A kindred of Lancea that she’d occasionally had spats with. She could respect his regard for more mortal matters, but like all Lancea was too concerned about the spiritual when there were many more ‘earthly’ problems to attend. Besides, he was a pig.
Ideally, the protest would gain fervor enough to force the Church of Saint Leon to capitulate, damaging the Lancea’s hold and reinforcing the Carthian’s in the process. 
The fire hadn’t been part of the plan.
A few hours into the protest the main spire of the church started to smoke as fire ignited throughout its finely decorated halls. Despite the precautions and panicked measures of kindred and kine, the church went from hosting congregations, to conflagrations. Any notion of protest was quashed as people rushed to help, calling the fire department and tending to wounded. The emergency services arrived in time to stop the fire from spreading but the church was incinerated down to its stone foundations. 
The fire’s origin was never identified. Most assumed a tragic accident, while the conspiracy minded suspected arson. Carthians presumed the involvement of another covenant, the Invictus working their shadowy influence or an enterprising Lanea member opening up some higher positions. Lancea of course held Carthians accountable- however badly the fire might have damaged their cause, as any scandal was quickly forgotten in favor of sympathy and donations to the burned church and its victims. 

The truth of it didn’t really matter, the fire had burnt a further rift between any semblance of kindred cooperation, and killed quite a few besides. Saint Leons would now always be associated with tragedy. 

To all but the AOA. 

The burning of Saint Leon's may have not been a Carthian plan, but the Anti-Obstructionist Alliance had spotted an opportunity to lighten the hash grip the Lancea had over the ideologically influential church. Too good an opportunity to pass up.

Thus, before the protest had gathered completely, René was to intercept a group of priests and dispose of them to allow a team of mortals to slip into the church disguised in their place to set the fire, and thereby burn the bodies. How the AOA managed to find a group of mortals ready to burn down a church, she wasn’t sure- weather it was money or for ideological reasons, she was vehemently encouraged not to ask. She suspected they would share the same fate as the priests after all was said. The death of a loose end, a worthy sacrifice for the cause of keeping mortal creativity unimpeded for the betterment of kindred and kine both.

The problem being, as she crouched outside the window of the parsonage which housed the gaggle of priests preparing to face the crowd, she was getting cold feet.

Of course many of them were under Lancea's influence, and she’d killed before, for her cause and many reasons besides- but these mortals were ignorant of the masters they served. Innocent tools. She had been told that no one who fosters great change has clean hands, but putting it into practice was another matter.
There was also the risk of being caught. She’d have no support- the AOA was disavowed by the Carthian Movement publically, she’d be branded a sole instigator and likely meet final death. She believed vehemently in what she was fighting for, wishing to honestly say she would sacrifice anything for it. She could not. Her fear was rousing the beast, her guilt was tugging at the parts of her that remembered being human. Disgusted with herself she caved, abandoning her post and betraying her colleagues.

And though her nerve had failed her, her luck had not.

As when she reached the tunnel that served as her escape route, she spied Charlie, whom she had worked with before on matters of unpleasantness. Whether he had business with the Lance or was just on his way to a job, didn’t matter- she was willing to pay a higher price for his help. 

Explaining what needed doing she begged Charlie into service- not telling all of it, but certainly telling too much. Be it the price she paid, her desperation, or a particular enmity for priests- he accepted, and she was released from this burden. When finally the smoking beginnings of a fire rose from the main spire of Saint Leon's her relief was matched only with her guilt; Not guilt over sentencing hapless mortals to their death, but rather a guilt borne of failing in her duty. She made a promise to herself then, that it would never happen again.

Months later, she received a painting in the style of a movement she had been fondly following- an indulgence she often partook in. It was sent by a kindred named ‘Keeper’ who had apparently extracted the cost of her vandalism upon this burgeoning movement- destroying all but one of their art pieces, and removing the creative spark that drove them. 
It was the greatest lesson in the terrible power of Kindred she’d ever received.
Terra Australis
Her proceeding work for the AOA was not as pivotal, René suspected some had sensed her waver in commitment. This doubt drove her to redouble efforts, even going so far as to find an Invictus Neonate who’d had their own doubts about their place in the Invictus. 
A capitalist to the quick, Eve was on the board of a company René had organised industrial action against in her mortal life. Bringing such a Neonate onside would be good evidence of her commitment- and with a few words and a bit of assurance of safety, Eve flipped Carthian. Of course, Eve’s concern was more so towards disrupting the power structure of elders and less about the ideological debate of the Movement- but they took in all stripes in the Movements, it was one of their biggest strengths. 
As time went on and her newly minted revolutionary tested the water of Kindred politics, she watched on with a distant hand to correct where needed- after all it didn’t count if they couldn’t figure it out themselves. No one could figure out how to make Eve a better Carthian than Eve-  With, of course, a touch of guidance. 
Once the neonate had learnt the ropes a little more, then she’d start introducing her more radical of sentiments… and perhaps they’d make a revolutionary of them yet.
René hadn’t the time to do so, as news that the kindred anathematic energies of Australia was receding- and she had been chosen to represent the AOA on these new shores.
Why she’d been picked, she couldn’t be sure. The preservation and study of this until recently vampire-free society was obviously important, but perhaps not as much as solidifying the hold of Anti-Obstructionist sentiment here, where all the powerful players moved. 
Truthfully, there was no guarantee they weren’t just looking to be rid of her. 
Regardless she would go, which meant wrapping up business in England for the foreseeable future. 
First and foremost, her Sire. Though she'd managed to gain distance from the blasé elder, she was still beholden to Oscar in many ways. If she was to go she would need his permission, and as was his habit she came to ask at a very convenient time for the man. 

He was looking to offload some inconvenient favours- and was rather curious about Australia besides. She could go, as long as she told him of the sights regularly… and took an old looking token safely on her person at all times. It was the representation of a favour she owed an elder associate of his, and she was to grant whomever gave its twin whatever they asked, within reason, though he did not specify what sort of reason it was within. 
With that, he gave a brief wave goodbye. Though she had no love for the man René felt a deep sense of melancholy to be so flippantly dismissed across the world by the person she gave up her life for, and had often worked so hard to please- but it was that sort of feeling that drove her to distance herself in the first place. Whatever the case, Oscar did not seem to care much more beyond that- though elders always had their tricks. Perhaps he planned to travel himself once the country was more open. 

Not a unique situation, probably.

Secondly, she’d been asked by the Movement to keep an eye on a newly awakened Elder, Einar Solveig. After a considerable time in torpor, their power had been much reduced- but they still had enough to completely eviscerate three ghouls that had been sent after him for some reason or another. Cut them in half apparently. Down the middle. 

Once more René was unsure if she was being given a powerful ally, or someone was trying to get her killed.

Nearly having failed this task as soon as it’d been assigned to her- it was only her luck and a few handy contacts that gave her warning that Einar and Eve were preparing to leave for Australia well in advance of what had been originally planned. Forced to improvise, she took what she could carry and rushed to the airport. Step one was to be invited on, step zero was being there to be invited in the first place. 

Some awkward maneuvering, and a much-too-lavish plane ride later, and they’d arrived on an island off the coast of Australia, ready to take the final stride to Terra Australis…
Mask: Nurturer
Public facing, to both kine and kindred to an extent- René is a supportive influence that despite her rough exterior, wants to see others grow into their best selves. In her mortal life, this was likely her true motivation- and is what drove her to become so involved in union life.
Durge: Idealist
In death, however, she is solely devoted to the cause of the Anti-Obstructionist Army. Kindred should have as little interference in mortal invention possible. Vampires are creatures of stasis, and to engage in the temptations of manipulation and domination will just stifle any advancement. Creating ghouls in particular is a temptation that they all needed to rise above, for the sake of all.
They were to be silent vigils- working on matters of the immortal soul, and perhaps rarely implementing and assisting with the new concepts and ideas of those who can still create without a beast clutching their hearts and minds.
So she will cut the strings of exploitation, perpetuate the masquerade, and, if they could not be weaned off their addiction, kill the ghouls that spread their masters' influence.
Character Connections

Einar Solveig (Hodge), Venture, Carthian- Coterie member and fellow Carthian outcast on the fringe.

Anichka (Katie), Mekhet, Crone- Fortuneteller she met in France, who told René glimpses of her future that she ignored.

Conrad Sondergaard (Arnold), Mekhet, Lance & London Lancea- Policeman and rival, had many conflicts in London and is likely the closest to uncovering her involvement in the Saint Leon’s fire.

Cyrus Renold (Bill), Daeva, Crone- Holder of the token that her Sire gave her, and will need to do him a Moderate boons worth of favour if he presents it to her. Their sires know each other.

Doc (Jess), Gangerl, Carthian & Macheath (Charlie), Mekhet, Carthian- Pair of Kindred IRA members who saved her after she got exploded by a ghoul she was targeting. Owes 1 Moderate boon to each of them.

Ivana Horvat (Lyn), Mekhet, Crone- Met during Ivana’s mortal days, saw René joining the AOA and will be very surprised to see her at court- if she even recognises Ivana at all.
