Charlie Ballsack (Kurtis), Nos, Invictus- Has worked with him before, and he’s cleaned up a few messes for René to her great shame. Likes to think they get along on at least a professional level. Owes him a moderate boon.

Keeper (Symon G)- Trashed their cults headquarters in London, and is still on the hook about it. Knows her association with the AOA.

Rene St. Dominique 

Rene is the eldest son of a prominent colonial French plantation owners and locksmith family who lived in Saint-Domingue up until the Haitian Revolution. 

As a child Rene was raised to believe his family were born to rule over lesser people and expected his life to be charmed. He took to the meticulous nature of locksmithing, enjoying the intricacies of the making of safes for banks, business and other families across Saint-Domingue. As a young man he began an unhealthy obsession with local customs, their bizarre gods they worshipped and their culture, much to his parents distaste.

As an adult he hosted balls and parties even while tensions continued to a boiling point between the slaves and forced labourers of the island. During these parties he became fascinated with a native man named Eloius, who seemed otherworldly around the otherwise mundane society types he surrounded himself with. Through late night discourse over several years Rene was seduced and became aware of the real nature of Eloius as a vampire of the Apollinaire bloodline, he explained the Loa & their goals to him. While Rene acted as a cover and go to agent, ensuring Eloius’ haven was upgraded with top of the line modern locks and protection. 

When the Revolution began Rene’s family were too stubborn to see the danger posed to them and continued to help finance the French colonial army right up until the collapse. They were caught up in the civilian massacres, only Rene was spared when Eloius intervened and provided him with protection. In exchange for his families property (including slaves) Eloius offered him an exchange to Embrace him. 

In the following years Rene assumed a new identity locally and continued to act as an agent for Eloius before being sent to France to continue on his service for Eloius and the Invictus in Paris, continuing his trade travelling through Europe acting as a locksmith and depending on circumstance a safe cracker. During both World Wars he acted as a vault keeper of important documents and items for both Kindred and Kine. 

Through the years he has acted as Notary for the Invictus as well as a collector of information on matters of dispute being able to communicate with ghosts of the long dead. His sire having traveled from Haiti to the United States and joining Rene in Paris, upon hearing of the returning of Kindred to Sydney he agreed to help spread the Invictus’ influence to the so far untouched domain.

In life I was a drifter, content with riding the tumultuous waves of the gig economy to whatever destination they brought me. A wrong turn in an area with spotty GPS was all it took for me to get a second chance.

Newly turned, I had the displeasure of being shown the proverbial ropes by a sire who somehow managed to achieve less in undeath than I had in my meagre life; they were a member of a group I would come to know as the Carthian movement, like their sire and their sire before them.

But where I previously found contentment in the struggle for the scraps of others I now only found bitterness and anger, at my sires lineage for squandering this gift, at myself for having the poor judgement to be reborn their childe. My blood called out to the Beast, and it helped me.

The first time the Beast's muzzle slipped off was when I fully realised what it meant to be a child of the night, it's when I truly felt Embraced. It proved to me that everything you want can be yours through blood sweat and toil, and that if you play your cards right, the blood doesn't even have to be your own.

With my new-found ambition, I set about building a new life for myself brick by brick, bone by bone. But I was immature then, my only experience with other vampires being my inept sire, so it didn't take long for me to step on the toes of someone who ACTUALLY meant something. I was enjoying the thrill of the Beast, and let it kill a kine in the feeding grounds of the person I would soon call my liege, and he was glorious. Aleksander Ambrose; Imagine a predator so refined it didn't have to bare its fangs for you to feel their threat, that through sheer force of will is capable of impressing upon you their grace as a benefactor and their ferocity as an adversary. I don't know what Aleksander saw in me, but it's by his grace that I found a new purpose, and in his image I saw my template. That is the night I sloughed off my disgraceful lineage, and started down a new path as a member of the Invictus.

I spent just over a year working as Aleksander's personal shit kicker, and fate has a sense of humour; I was his food delivery driver. Eventually though I ingratiated myself enough with him that he felt comfortable entrusting me with a more important matter.

I now find myself comfortable on a private jet owned by Aleksander. I am headed to Sydney, a place he believes will become a hotbed of kindred activity. He says it will be decades if not centuries until it becomes clear who will control Sydney's streets, but during my nights in Sydney I am to connect with other members of my covenant and prepare for our master's future rule. We Invictus are not here to ask for a throne, we are here to build one.

Though as the nights continue to pass, I find myself wondering if perhaps I should be the one to experience the boons of my labour...

Tegan Groves was born in Hobart, Tasmania in 1975. Her father was a park ranger at Mount Field National Park, and her mother is a housewife. Her sister Tahliah was born in 1978. 

Tegan was raised in the great outdoors. Her toys were sticks and leaves and the mud that stuck on the floors of Huon Pine forests. When she was 13 she was bitten by a Copperhead Snake while hiking and exploring with her sister and dad. Being from a smaller town near Hobart, there wasn't much to do for teenagers and she started drinking at 14. 

She was an average student who preferred being outside, so she left school in year 10 and hopped around retail jobs. She lived with her parents and used her money to travel, visiting Melbourne, Sydney, Gold Coast, Bali, Hong Kong, and Singapore before saving up to travel to India. 


At 23, Tegan worked as a check out lady at Target in Hobart during the day and went out with work mates and old school friends at night. 

At 25, Tegan travelled to India. Her first stop: the beautiful and fast-paced coastal city and "Gateway to India": Mumbai. On her third night in Mumbai Tegan was lured to a dark bungalow in an overgrown forest where she met a man who called himself Brigadier-General Tenley and after she was attacked and fed on, she was given a choice: be embraced, or die. 
She chose to live. She met "the troops"; a collection of neophytes from around the world, all of whom answered to the Brigadier's whims. They protected the territory, brought unsuspecting victims, cleaned, negotiated with other kindred, fought with each other, and entertained their sire.

The Brigadier made sure to tell them all why they had been chosen; they were sinners. Flotsam, drunkards, harlots, ne'er-do-wells who would be put to good use now under his watchful eye. They had all been Christian in life, and would now be Lancea in death. They would test those who fled from God's country into a heathen land, and he himself would be their punishment or redemption. Why him? Oh, because even the heathens themselves had seen the divinity within and revered him as a god. Sure, his followers had dwindled since the 1930's but there was still memory of "Teyn Li". And he had not forgotten it. 

 Tegan picked up Hindi, learned how to use shadows and streets to lose tails and follow strangers, and through manipulation of a tenuous pecking order, became a trusted favourite of the Brigadier. So when they heard that Australia was suddenly "open", she was his first pick, much to the jealousy of the others. 

Australia has afforded Tegan freedom she hasn't felt for 25 years. It drowns out any worries she may have about other kindred, but she knows she must keep the Brigadier's trust and do as he has commanded lest he send another neophyte to take her place.

YUSUF'S TIMELINE
~1896, Ottoman borderlands (Turkey/Greece region): Yusuf is a mortal infantry soldier running a smuggling ring. Unbeknownst to him, the smuggling operation is actually an Invictus business venture. A beautiful female Invictus vampire becomes infatuated with him — he's handsome and effective at what he does. She begins feeding on him; he thinks it's just sex. Her obsession deepens until she decides she wants to both sire him and marry him. Before the wedding, she conspires with a local Circle of the Crone cell to perform a ritual that allows her (a vampire) to conceive a child — a dhampir. She gets political clearance from the Invictus because Yusuf is seen as a genuinely useful asset worth bringing into the fold.
The Wedding Night, ~1896: The plan is to marry Yusuf and then Embrace him on the marital bed. The wedding goes ahead. On the wedding night, just as she's about to sire him, a Dominate command kicks in and she freezes, unable to complete the Embrace. Out of the shadows steps a Gangrel from a rival Invictus faction (a coterie-mate of this Gangrel's sire), laughing. The Gangrel sires Yusuf instead — making him Nosferatu. The beautiful man is now a monster. This was a calculated power play and humiliation of the bride and her faction.
The Fallout: The Gangrel takes his new childe and leaves. The bride is now pregnant with Yusuf's dhampir child AND completely unhinged by what's been done to her. Her sire has egg on his face politically. In retaliation, her faction orchestrates the ultimate revenge: they force Yusuf to commit Amaranth (diablerie) — consuming his own sire's soul. Then, instead of executing Yusuf for the crime of diablerie (which would normally be the punishment), he is staked and sealed in a box — torpor as a living coffin. There was some intervention on the bride's part that prevented his Final Death; the "best case scenario" was imprisonment rather than destruction. This all wraps up around 1900.
~1897: The dhampir child is born. The bride's situation in Turkey/Istanbul becomes untenable, so she and the child are shipped off to the Americas (eventually ending up in the New Orleans/Louisiana region). The child is raised at arm's length from the vampire community — protected but not enmeshed in Kindred society.
The Generational Line: The dhampir child (born ~1897) grows up, has a mortal child (~1920s), who has a mortal child, and so on. Each generation the vampiric blood dilutes further. Eventually, several generations down, Belle/Brandy LaRue is born — fully mortal, no knowledge of her supernatural heritage, but the spitting image of the original Invictus bride. This resemblance is why the bride (still active in the Americas) plans to Embrace Belle — essentially creating a new version of herself.

THE MNEMOSYNE'S INTERVENTION
~1930s, Istanbul: The city has been ravaged by war. There are numerous torpid vampires sealed away, and wartime destruction has obliterated the records of who they are and why they were boxed. The Invictus brings in the Mnemosyne — a specialist who can enter torpid vampires' minds and read their memories to catalogue them.
The Mnemosyne enters Yusuf's mind and gorges on the trauma — it's incredible psychic material. Crucially, he steals the memory of the Amaranth. Yusuf no longer remembers consuming his sire's soul. The Mnemosyne then lies to the Invictus, telling them Yusuf was just a neonate who was put on ice during wartime chaos as a precaution, never meant to be boxed this long, simple administrative error. They dust Yusuf off, wake him, and reintegrate him into the Istanbul Invictus court.
The Mnemosyne also creates an artificial blood tie with Yusuf by tasting his blood while he was torpid. This allows the Mnemosyne to track and check in on Yusuf indefinitely — like a producer keeping tabs on the star of his show.
The Mnemosyne also fuzzes Yusuf's memory of his bride's face. Not a complete erasure — Yusuf knows he had a bride, knows she was taken from him, but can't quite picture her clearly anymore. He has a painting of her, but when he looks at it, the face doesn't fully resolve in his memory. This is why Belle can be the spitting image of the bride and Yusuf feels an overwhelming pull toward her without being able to articulate exactly why.

THE MOVE TO AMERICA
The Mnemosyne begins slowly leaking breadcrumbs to Yusuf — not about Belle, but about the fact that his bride may still be alive (when Yusuf assumed she was truly dead). This sends Yusuf on a wandering trail that eventually leads him to America.
Meanwhile, the Gangrel coterie-mate of Yusuf's sire (still carrying a grudge and wanting to curry favour) has also followed the bride's lineage to the Americas. This Gangrel eventually sires a rich Southern magnate to bolster the local Invictus. That new childe (the Gangrel's grandchilde, effectively) wants to climb the Invictus ladder and finds a way to do it: he locates Yusuf's ex-bride, stakes her, and presents her as a gift to his sire — tying off the old grudge. This earns him status. He then spends his time torturing Belle as a kind of neglected, abused childe/captive — the "trailer trash" of his household.
Yusuf arrives in America (New Orleans/Louisiana area) just as this is all playing out. He gets there essentially as the ramifications are echoing — too late to intervene in his bride's staking, but in time to witness the aftermath. This is a breaking point for him. He's been manipulated, his bride has been taken from him again, and the Invictus power structure enabled all of it.

THE CARTHIAN TURN
This is the moment Yusuf breaks from the Invictus and joins the local Carthian Movement. It's not ideology at first — it's visceral rage at a system that has repeatedly destroyed everything he cares about. But once among the Carthians, he starts to absorb a different philosophy. He sees vampires who disagree violently but don't kill each other. Who argue and punch it out rather than orchestrating centuries-long revenge cycles. It infects him with the idea that it doesn't have to be the way it's always been.

BELLE'S ESCAPE AND PROTECTION
Belle (as the captive of the Gangrel's grandchilde) begins sneaking out to spend time with the Circle of the Crone. Her captor doesn't care at first — it means he doesn't have to deal with her. But when she stops coming back entirely, he gets angry and goes to reclaim her. The Hierophant of the local Crone circle teaches him a lesson, and he backs off, deciding she's not worth the trouble. But the threat remains.
Yusuf, now Carthian and operating from the shadows, becomes aware of Belle through the court — she's presented publicly at some point. He recognises that she is the spitting image of the painting he carries, the face he can't quite remember. He doesn't know why this matters so much, but something primal in him demands he protect her. He begins shadow-guarding her without her knowledge — never approaching, never revealing himself. The Nosferatu in the shadows, watching over someone who doesn't know he exists.

THE MOVE TO SYDNEY
The Mnemosyne orchestrates the final act. He's being sent to Sydney (attached to Curtis's character as a kind of kept agent — too useful to kill, too unstable to leave unsupervised). Knowing both Yusuf and Belle are in the Americas, he pulls strings:
He nudges Belle's Crone Hierophant to send Belle to Sydney — framing it as both an opportunity and a way to get her away from the ongoing threat of her old captor. The Crone's own divinations confirm it's genuinely good for Belle, so she goes willingly.
Yusuf, still shadow-tracking Belle, sees her leave for Sydney. He uses the Carthian expedition as his cover — he's going to Sydney to export the Mammon cult, establish a communal feeding resource for the Carthian Movement. Legitimate Carthian business. But his real motivation is following Belle.
The Mnemosyne has now manoeuvred all three of them into the same fishbowl — Sydney — with no other established vampires, no old power structures to hide behind, and nowhere to run. Season two of his favourite show.

WHAT YUSUF KNOWS vs. DOESN'T KNOW
Knows: He had a bride. She was taken from him on their wedding night. His sire is dead (knows this through Blood Sympathy). He was boxed and later freed in Istanbul. He spent time in the Invictus, then broke to the Carthians. Belle looks exactly like the painting he carries of his bride.
Doesn't know: He committed Amaranth (memory stolen by the Mnemosyne). The exact face of his bride (memory fuzzed). That Belle is his biological great-granddaughter through the dhampir line. That the Mnemosyne has been orchestrating events around him for decades. That the Mnemosyne holds stolen memories and may have planted triggers — possibly including a command that if Yusuf ever kisses Belle, the memory of devouring his sire's soul comes flooding back.

THE HIDDEN BOMB
The Mnemosyne has potentially implanted the stolen Amaranth memory back into Yusuf's mind, locked behind a trigger: physical intimacy with Belle (specifically, their lips touching). If that ever happens, Yusuf doesn't remember kissing his great-granddaughter — he remembers consuming his sire's soul. The Mnemosyne is essentially waiting for this moment like it's a season finale.
